data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260515T070047-706bc975/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260515T070047-706bc975/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260515T070047-706bc975/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260515T070047-706bc975/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260515T070047-706bc975/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-e4c3d791">
    <title>ddx home: presence-based root resolution + per-project worktree registry: part 4</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-06cbaa90 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 4 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM (Principle 1 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

Eliminate hardcoded .ddx/ paths so that DDx state anchors correctly regardless of whether the project has a .ddx/ folder. Today every code path joins filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", ...) directly, so introducing an XDG fallback or any other root strategy means a many-callsite rewrite.

DESIGN

ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot) is the single accessor. It returns:
- <projectRoot>/.ddx — when that directory exists (in-tree mode)
- ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/ — otherwise (convention mode)

Project identity in convention mode is parsed from `git remote get-url origin`:
- https://github.com/easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx
- git@github.com:easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx
- https://gitlab.example.com/team/repo → gitlab.example.com/team/repo
- Local (no remote) → local/<basename>-<short-hash-of-abs-path>

The XDG project directory IS a git repository — `git init`ed on first bootstrap. All DDx state for that project (beads.jsonl, attachments, archive, plugins, etc.) lives there and is versioned. Operators can `git remote add` it to a server to share state with teammates.

WORKTREE REGISTRY (required, not speculative)

Each XDG project root maintains <xdg>/worktrees.json:

  {"paths": [{"path": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx", "first_seen_at": "...", "last_seen_at": "...", "hostname": "..."}], "master": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx"}

- The first worktree to bootstrap an XDG project is auto-marked master.
- Subsequent worktrees register as non-master.
- `ddx project worktree list` shows the registry.
- `ddx project worktree master <path>` re-pins.
- Audit log entries (bead events with result_rev/base_rev) include the master path so downstream tools resolve commit SHAs to a known worktree.

DDX_ROOT NEVER PUNNED — bootstrap is atomic, idempotent, and a no-op when the directory already exists. Two concurrent ddx invocations on the same project must not race-create or race-write worktrees.json. Use file-locking (existing gitlock pattern from ddx-6a518fd9) where needed.

AUDIT — every Go file in cli/ that contains the literal ".ddx" as a path segment must route through ddxroot.Path. Touched packages at minimum:
- cli/cmd/ (every command that reads/writes state)
- cli/internal/agent/ (execute_bead*, work/, escalation/)
- cli/internal/bead/ (store, migrate, lifecycle)
- cli/internal/serv
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- TestWorktreeRegistry_SubsequentRegisterNonMaster asserts later worktrees register but do not overwrite master.
- TestWorktreeRegistry_OperatorRePin asserts `ddx project worktree master <path>` updates the pin.
- Static check via lefthook: `rg 'filepath\.Join\([^)]*"\.ddx"' cli/ --type go | grep -v '^cli/internal/ddxroot/'` returns zero hits. The lint rule is wired into pre-commit.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. TestWorktreeRegistry_SubsequentRegisterNonMaster asserts later worktrees register but do not overwrite master.
2. TestWorktreeRegistry_OperatorRePin asserts `ddx project worktree master <path>` updates the pin.
3. Static check via lefthook: `rg 'filepath\.Join\([^)]*"\.ddx"' cli/ --type go | grep -v '^cli/internal/ddxroot/'` returns zero hits. The lint rule is wired into pre-commit.
    </acceptance>
    <notes>
decomposed into ddx-fd98ffaa (registry CLI/tests), ddx-b6fea19a (cmd/config/git audit), ddx-3bbf066b (agent/exec audit), ddx-16fcc557 (server/graphql audit), ddx-2f93f5d9 (test sweep + pre-commit audit)
    </notes>
    <labels>area:config, area:refactor, kind:refactor, phase:3, initiative:zero-footprint, principle:ddx-home, decomposed</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-06cbaa90" base-rev="cbf10e4a1b29782d1a46a8791d427aa9a158cfdc" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260515T070047-706bc975"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Implemented and committed as `bcedc270f`.

The acceptance slice is covered by the new worktree CLI in [project.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-e4c3d791-20260515T070047-706bc975/cli/cmd/project.go), the operator re-pin test in [project_worktree_test.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-e4c3d791-20260515T070047-706bc975/cli/cmd/project_worktree_test.go), the subsequent non-master registration test in [ddxroot_test.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-e4c3d791-20260515T070047-706bc975/cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot_test.go), and the pre-commit path audit wired in [lefthook.yml](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-e4c3d791-20260515T070047-706bc975/lefthook.yml). The `cli/` sweep removed hardcoded `.ddx` path joins outside `cli/internal/ddxroot/`.

Verification passed via `lefthook run pre-commit`, and the commit hook reran the full gated suite, including the package-by-package `go test -short -race` pass.
